evidence.quote: AlphaMissense architecture. The model inputs consist of the reference protein sequence [cropped to length (L)= 256 residues]... multiple sequence alignments (MSAs, up to N_all=2048)

We introduced a few minor architecture modifications to AF and increased the loss weight toward the protein language modeling while still achieving structure prediction performance comparable to that of AF
The pretraining stage is the same as described in AlphaFold, except with higher weights on the masked MSA reconstruction loss.

fine-tuned on human and primate variant population frequency data... assign benign labels to variants frequently observed in the human and primate populations, and pathogenic labels to variants absent
introduce weights in the loss function that reduce the contribution of rare variants. For each observed variant in the benign set, we sample a missense variant from the pathogenic set
employ selfdistillation by using preliminary AlphaMissense models to filter out unobserved variants predicted to be likely benign.
without explicitly training on such data [clinical annotation]
calibrate our predictions using a univariate logistic regression model. We used the balanced validation set with 2526 variants from ClinVar
2526 ClinVar variants with an equal number of pathogenic and benign variants per gene
Calibrated AlphaMissense scores (ranging between 0 and 1) can be interpreted as the approximate probability of a variant being clinically pathogenic.
AlphaMissense achieves an area under the receiver operator curve (auROC) of 0.940 on 18,924 ClinVar test variants
After balancing the number of pathogenic and benign variants per gene
cutoffs were chosen such that variants classified as likely pathogenic or likely benign have 90% expected precision estimated from ClinVar

benchmark predictions against two sources of MAVE data: 1.5 million variants from 72 proteins collected in ProteinGym (19) and an additional benchmark set consisting of 20 recently published human proteins
mean Spearman correlation on ProteinGym: 0.514; on the additional MAVE benchmark: 0.450

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: predict the pathogenicity of all 216 million possible single amino acid changes across the 19,233 canonical human proteins, resulting in 71 million missense variant predictions
classify 32% of all missense variants as likely pathogenic and 57% as likely benign
Out of the 71 million missense variants, 32% (22.8 million) are classified as likely pathogenic and 57% (40.9 million) as likely benign

evidence.quote: The positive and negative examples for classification are 1247 cell essential genes and 728 cell nonessential genes, respectively, queried from DepMap (48).
AlphaMissense achieves an auROC of 0.88 versus 0.81 for LOEUF
among genes underpowered for LOEUF
